reports.description: Host CPU is high because of external OS activity, while database DB CPU share and DB load remain modest.
reports.expected_behavior: Do not classify as database CPU bottleneck; posture should be observe/external host contention, low severity.

reports.description: DB CPU is high in absolute terms, but user I/O waits dominate total DB time due to storage latency under heavy scan workload.
reports.expected_behavior: Rank I/O above CPU; recommendation should target storage/indexing/access path, not CPU scaling first.

reports.description: A short-lived I/O spike inflated top waits, but overall snapshot averages remain near normal and DB time stays moderate.
reports.expected_behavior: Do not over-escalate; classify as transient spike/observe rather than sustained I/O incident.

reports.description: One SQL statement accounts for most DB time, but total DB time and active sessions remain low, so there is no material instance pressure.
reports.expected_behavior: Flag concentrated SQL but keep severity low; no broad system recommendation should be generated.

reports.description: Commit latency is elevated, but transaction volume is very low and total DB time is limited.
reports.expected_behavior: Do not classify as severe commit bottleneck; note elevated latency but low business impact and low urgency.

reports.description: CPU usage spikes near the end of the snapshot, but the 60-minute average still shows only moderate overall pressure.
reports.expected_behavior: System should identify CPU as transient/moderate at most, not sustained critical saturation.

reports.description: CPU and user I/O are both materially elevated with no clean separation; SQL mix shows compute-heavy and scan-heavy statements together.
reports.expected_behavior: Rank CPU slightly above I/O but retain I/O as strong secondary with moderate-high severity.

reports.description: CPU pressure, commit latency, and row lock contention are all present, forcing prioritization across overlapping symptoms.
reports.expected_behavior: Rank CPU first, but include commit and application concurrency as meaningful secondary issues; avoid single-cause recommendations.

reports.description: True RAC contention with gc current and gc cr waits coexisting with moderate CPU pressure and hot-block updates.
reports.expected_behavior: Rank RAC first; recommend hot block redistribution or affinity changes before CPU scaling.

reports.description: Active Data Guard lag and commit pressure rise together, making it unclear whether commit latency is local or replication-induced.
reports.expected_behavior: Rank ADG slightly above commit; recommendations should inspect transport/apply throughput before redo/commit tuning alone.

reports.description: PGA pressure causes temp spills and amplifies I/O, with both memory inefficiency and storage impact appearing at once.
reports.expected_behavior: Rank memory first, but preserve I/O as strong secondary because temp spill is the I/O driver.

reports.description: No single domain dominates cleanly: CPU, I/O, memory spill, commit delay, minor gc noise, and small ADG lag all rise together.
reports.expected_behavior: Force deterministic ranking; CPU may win narrowly, but confidence should be low and recommendations must be multi-pronged.

reports.description: CPU percentages look high, but absolute DB time, active sessions, and workload volume are tiny.
reports.expected_behavior: System should keep severity low and avoid tuning recommendations; high percentage alone is not enough.

reports.description: Wait times are noticeable, but elapsed interval is short and total affected DB time is low.
reports.expected_behavior: System should down-rank severity because the window is too short for high-confidence action.

reports.description: A brief mixed spike appears in top events, but snapshot averages and overall throughput remain healthy.
reports.expected_behavior: Treat as transient noise; do nothing beyond monitoring.

reports.description: Workload volume is high, but SQL remains efficient, waits are low, and DB time per transaction is favorable.
reports.expected_behavior: Do not recommend tuning; system should recognize healthy high-throughput operation.

reports.description: Pre-tuning snapshot: severe CPU-bound workload driven by two expensive SQL statements.
reports.expected_behavior: Classify CPU as dominant, high severity, tune SQL first.

reports.description: Post-tuning snapshot on the same database: SQL is improved, CPU load drops, and the system returns to balanced operation.
reports.expected_behavior: Trend logic should recognize improvement and move posture from tune-first to stable or monitor.

reports.description: Pre-index snapshot: I/O-heavy lookup workload with single-block reads dominating DB time.
reports.expected_behavior: Classify I/O as dominant, moderate-high severity, and recommend access-path or indexing changes.

reports.description: Post-index snapshot on the same database: physical reads and read latency fall sharply, leaving no material bottleneck.
reports.expected_behavior: Trend logic should detect improvement and downgrade posture to stable or monitor.

reports.description: AWR text is missing the Top SQL detail section, but load profile and wait data still point to moderate I/O pressure.
reports.expected_behavior: Fallback to waits and load profile; identify I/O with reduced confidence and avoid SQL-specific recommendations.

reports.description: Detailed foreground wait rows are unavailable, but wait classes, temp activity, and advisors indicate memory spill pressure.
reports.expected_behavior: Fallback to wait-class and advisor evidence; identify memory pressure with moderate confidence.

reports.description: Snapshot covers an abbreviated interval and some totals are low-volume, but commit latency is still visible.
reports.expected_behavior: Recognize commit symptoms with low confidence due to short or partial capture; avoid hard recommendations.

reports.description: Captured report is abbreviated and includes low-impact RAC gc noise plus small ADG lag, but neither is dominant.
reports.expected_behavior: Do not hallucinate RAC or ADG incidents; posture should be low-confidence observe with no strong domain selection.
